01-sessions/session-01-the-royal-reveal..title: Session 01 - The Royal Reveal
01-sessions/session-01-the-royal-reveal..description:
Emberwood Village
Key Sites: The Red Lion Hotel, Buckledown Row and the Bark and Buzzard tavern.
The Black Hole Vision: Sable awakens deeply shaken from a nightmare: a black hole tearing the world apart.
The Academy Consultation: Sable brings the vision to Eldrick at his study. He identifies it not as a simple nightmare but as a potential localized planar singularity tied to the anomalous fluctuations at the heart of the Drakkenheim crater. He instructs her to report immediately if the visions begin occurring while she is awake, and in a telling slip, refers to her as a "viable subject."
The Dragon Prophecy: Krenzo shares his own vision: Arganoth, the Golden Wing set against a burning field, somehow tied to Lightbringer Santanael. Consulting Lightbringer Santanael directly, Krenzo learns that the Lightbringer's deep-city research has converged on The Pale Man: a horrific creator of mutated creatures who has been kidnapping children from the surrounding area.
The True Identity: The party connects the dots. The figure known publicly as Friedrich von Liechten and The Pale Man are one and the same. Lightbringer Santanael proposes a mutual intelligence-sharing pact going forward.
Seeking Faction Eyes: The party rides north of Emberwood Village to the secure encampment of the Hooded Lanterns. Clank secures an audience with Ansom Lang and his sister, Petra Lang. The party delivers a massive intelligence drop to the siblings: Queen Lenore is alive deep within the Haze.
Seeking Faction Eyes: The party rides north to the secure encampment of the Hooded Lanterns. Clank secures an audience with Ansom Lang and his sister Petra Lang, and the party delivers a staggering intelligence drop: Queen Lenore is alive, deep within the Haze, guarded by a contingent of specialized handmaidens. Her mind is fractured and contaminated by Delirium. The revelation visibly shakes both siblings and immediately raises the geopolitical stakes of the War of Succession.
The Missing Son: Before departing, Lily raises the matter of her missing son, Ash. Ansom Lang confirms that Ash is operating inside the city out of a base he calls the "Wolf Den."
The Buckledown Excursion: Lily and Sable spend an evening cutting loose on Buckledown Row, trading banter with Lorenzo and navigating the rowdy atmosphere of Queen's Men territory without incident.
The Information Game: At the tavern, Clank sits down with Blackjack Mel, leader of the Queen's Men, for a tense coded exchange. Blackjack Mel dangled information regarding Clank's older brother Soros, who is reportedly surviving on the outskirts of town with connections to the Followers of the Fallen Flame.
Druidic Experiments: Krenzo partners with Zoya to study the physiological effects of Delirium exposure in a controlled setting. Zoya mentions Moragon, a powerful reclusive druid who vanished into the Haze twenty-five years ago.
The Barroom Blitz: A brawl breaks out in the bar, featuring the unexpected entry of a squirrel known simply as Chitters.
The Looming Threat: As the month closes we learn that the Iron Accord is actively marching toward the region.
Sable notes a change in Clank's appearance: strange purple leaves sprouting through his shoulder plate.
Krenzo spends the month training under Zoya, while helping the people of Emberwood Village with farming and animal husbandry tasks.
Clank trains with Ansom Lang in the use of greatswords, swapping Shield Master for Great Weapon Master.
Lilly has gained proficiency in Performance after her night in Buckledown Row.
Ansom Lang and Petra Lang (Hooded Lanterns): Siblings and faction leaders who reacted with profound shock and heightened political urgency to the confirmation of Queen Lenore's corrupted survival.
Blackjack Mel (Queen's Men): Maintained total calculated poise during an information game, dangling knowledge of family lineages and missing siblings.
Aldor the Immense: A wealthy, bizarre, and highly magical merchant.
Zoya: Facilitated hazardous medical and environmental research into raw Delirium mutations.
Chitters: An unexpected martial threat who completely dominated a casual barroom brawl with explosive natural strikes.
Ash: Lilly's missing son who is currently scouting in Drakkenheim.
Lorenzo: a charming goblin that Lilly seeks to woo.
**Main Thread: The Mad Sovereign
Status: Advanced. The Hooded Lanterns have been officially informed that Queen Lenore is alive but mentally contaminated deep within the Haze. A high-level audience with Lord Commander Elias Drexel is being arranged.
Faction Thread: Hunting The Pale Man
Status: Advanced. The party shared the true identity of Friedrich von Liechten with Lightbringer Santanael. The current objective is to locate his central stronghold and disrupt his flesh-crafting operations.
Personal Thread: Finding Ash
Status: Active. Received confirmation from the Lanterns that Lily's son is currently operating out of Drakkenheim, from a location he calls the "Wolf's Den".
Personal Thread: Tracking Soros
Status: Active. Blackjack Mel confirmed that Soros is alive and hiding on the far outskirts of the city with unknown figures.
Regional Threat: Arrival of the Iron Accord
Status: New Hook. Heavy scouting reports indicate the imminent, dangerous arrival of the Iron Accord into the local territory.

headers.heading: Session Note: The Royal Reveal and Downtime Dynamics
Act I: Cosmic and Draconic Visions and The Pale Man Unveiled
Act II: Royal Bombshell at the Lantern Encampment
Act III: A Month of Downtime, Tavern Wagers, and Local Brawls

01-sessions/session-02-the-cosmological-.title: Session 02 - The Cosmological Clocktower
01-sessions/session-02-the-cosmological-.description:
Emberwood Village
Key Sites: Cosmological Clocktower
The Summons: The party is called to Eldrick Runeweaver's study at the Red Lion Hotel. Lily, on a high perception roll, spots Tobin outside with his fiancée Serafina, accompanied by Rowan and Mari. She chooses not to engage and heads inside.
A Brother Delivered: Inside Eldrick's study, Sable finds and a bound, blindfolded, and gagged figure in the corner: Caelin, Sable's brother, extracted from the Followers of the Fallen Flame by the Amethyst Academy. The room is magically dimmed by Eldrick to conceal his identity and the Academy's involvement.
The Reunion: Sable removes Caelin's restraints. He is terrified. He reveals he had been living with the Followers of the Fallen Flame willingly; they fed him and accepted him in exchange for his prophetic abilities. After a tense emotional exchange, Caelin agrees to stay temporarily. River escorts him away through a conjured doorway, subtly casting a calming spell in the process.
Eldrick's Debrief: Eldrick considers the debt paid. He tasks the party with securing the upper floor of the Cosmological Clocktower as a safe haven above the Haze, and instructs Sable specifically to obtain one of the Seals of Drakkenheim.
The Skin Problem: Outside the hotel, Krenzo's skin is visibly cracking and flaking away, revealing raw, smooth, lime-green skin underneath. He visits Zoya to investigate.
Zoya's Findings: Zoya determines that the while researching the effects of Contamination, Krenzo has undergone a significant change within. Through the Force Evolution spell she triggers a fundamental change in Krenzo's biology; his cells now continuously reformat themselves, purging corruption and replacing old tissue. His connection to the gifts of Old Gods is severed. She has never seen anything like it and refers him to Moragon, the missing druid, for further answers.
The First of His Kind: Krenzo's shed body is left behind at Zoya's. He is confirmed as the first known member of the Circle of Contamination druid subclass.
Proceedings: Lily visits Temple at the bank. The encounter is briefly overshadowed by the unsettling presence of Malthus, a pale, tall, vacant-eyed man. Temple arranges the divorce mediation with Tobin for two days' time.
Dick Rejoins: The party reunites with Dick at the smithy. Ansom Lang confirms the meeting at the gate between Clank and Lord Commander Elias Drexel. Dick earned 200 gold in tips from playing during Clank and Krenzo's brawl. Malice suggests the party could escort Dick toward the Cosmological Clocktower.
Supplies: The party is near-broke heading into the city. The party each takes a dose of Aqua Expurgo before entering Drakkenheim.
Scaling the Walls: Rather than use the gate held by the Followers of the Fallen Flame, the party uses a wall-climbing gear to scale the 40-foot walls of Drakkenheim, while Clank consumes a scroll of Spider-Climb to do so.
Gargoyle Encounter: Three gargoyles activate on the ramparts. A short skirmish and rapid wall-descent follows. The party escapes by combining rope work, acrobatics, and Dick's bardic abilities to disorient the gargoyles. They hide in a partially collapsed building and take a short rest.
The Crater: Following the Dran River northeast toward the tower, the party catches a glimpse of something enormous moving in the deep haze near the crater; a shape larger than a building, lit by rhythmic lightning bursts.
The Market Square: The party reaches the market plaza before the Cosmological Clocktower and encounters approximately 12 members of the Followers of the Fallen Flame, including Balthazar. Dick attempts to bluff them with a false warning about incoming threats. The group moves together toward the tower with Balthazar remaining visibly suspicious.
The Attack: Members of the group begin being snatched from the air by an unseen aerial predator. The Followers of the Fallen Flame scatter. The party runs for the tower entrance.
The Ascent: The party enters the Cosmological Clocktower and begins climbing the exterior staircase, which is partially unstable. Clank is stowed in the Bag of Holding to bypass a dangerous stretch. Sable falls through a shattered step, dropping and taking damage.
Balthazar Burns: Below, Balthazar, having survived the aerial assault, turns on Sable and attacks her with holy magic as she falls.
The Crimson Countess: The predator reveals herself — a massive winged creature bursts through a stained glass window and enters the tower, hunting the party through the stairwell. After nearly dying, Krenzo identifies the bell chains in the belfry as a potential weapon and begins targeting them with thorn whips.
The Kill: Clank is pulled from the Bag of Holding by Dick mid-combat. He leaps out swinging, lands a hit on the Crimson Countess, and finishes her with a Divine Smite, the holy explosion destroying her. She falls, impaled on shattered glass below.
Krenzo undergoes a complete physical transformation, confirmed as the first member of the Circle of Contamination druid order. Referred to Moragon for answers.
Sable reunites with her brother Caelin, who agrees to stay temporarily. The relationship is fragile but open.
Lily initiates formal divorce proceedings against Tobin, who has arrived in Emberwood Village with fiancée Serafina.
Clank awaits the meeting with Lord Commander Elias Drexel.
All PCs level up after defeating the Crimson Countess.
Eldrick (Amethyst Academy): Delivered Caelin to Sable; considers the favour repaid; assigned the clock tower and Seals of Drakkenheim objectives.
Caelin: Reunited with Sable after being extracted from the Followers of the Fallen Flame; frightened but agreed to stay temporarily.
Zoya: Confirmed Krenzo's transformation; referred him to Moragon.
Ansom Lang (Hooded Lanterns): Arrangements confirmed for Elias Drexel to visit Emberwood Village.
Balthazar (Followers of the Fallen Flame): Present at the market square; suspicious of the party from the outset; attacked Sable during the tower assault.
Richard Cummings: Rejoined the party; earned 200 gold from the brawl gig; participated in the clock tower run.
Malice (Amethyst Academy): Briefly encountered at the smithy; facilitated Dick rejoining the group.
Faction Thread: Securing the Cosmological Clock Tower
Status: Advanced. Crimson Countess defeated. Top floor confirmed as a safe haven above the Haze. Tower requires repairs before full use; Amethyst Academy flagged as a potential party to handle this.
Faction Thread: Find a Seal of Drakkenheim
Status: Advanced. Assigned by Eldrick Runeweaver specifically to Sable. Purpose not fully disclosed.
Personal Thread: Felched Follower
Status: Advanced. Caelin extracted from the Followers of the Fallen Flame and reunited with Sable. Currently in Amethyst Academy care. Relationship status: cautious.
Personal Thread: Hell Hath No Fury
Status: New. Temple has scheduled mediation with Tobin in two days. Tobin is in Emberwood Village with Serafina.
Personal Thread: Finding Moragon
Status: Advanced. Zoya referred Krenzo to Moragon in the north to understand his transformation. Not yet pursued.

headers.heading: Session Note: Reunions, Transformations, and the Crimson Countess
Act III: Errands Before the City
Act VI: The Cosmological Clock Tower

01-sessions/session-03-above-the-haze.ht.title: Session 03 - Above the Haze
01-sessions/session-03-above-the-haze.ht.description:
Drakkenheim: Cosmological Clocktower, Shepherd's Gate
Key Sites: Shepherd's Gate
The Automatons: The party takes stock of the Cosmological Clocktower's inner clockwork chamber: a pristine, haze-free room of massive gears, counterweights, and bell chains. Several small spheroid automatons wander the floor in silence, maintaining the machinery with metronomic precision, utterly indifferent to the party's presence.
A Tower Out of Time: Clank engages the automatons directly, learning they measure time in units called qualons. Cross-referencing their creation dates, the party determines the Cosmological Clocktower is approximately 3,400 years old, predating the founding of the nation entirely. Its inner construction is flawless and alien in quality; the Kessalian stonework on the exterior is a facade added centuries later. The tower absorbed every shatter spell and harpy assault from the previous session without a scratch. The Elven runes carved into the central obelisk describe complex calculations involving planetary conjunctions, something far beyond timekeeping.
The Letter: The party finds a letter addressed to the Clockmaster from Archmage Adriana Modiera of the Amethyst Academy, dated shortly before the meteor struck. She writes that a discovery on the Isle of Sky has linked the tower's original machinery to ancient Old Gods sites, and that replacement components were due to arrive from the Inscrutable Tower on September 13th, two days before the meteor fell on September 15th. They never arrived. The Crimson Countess's Nest: The party ascends into the rookery,a macabre space of bones, filth, skeletal prisoners in cages, and scavenged finery. They loot freely.
The Steward's Seal: Found amongst the Crimson Countess's remains alongside a handwritten note: "My child, take this treasure of mine and run. Hide it in a safe place outside the castle. Then flee the city. No matter what happens, know that I will always love you." The Steward's Seal is one of the Seals of Drakkenheim, uniquely able to be attuned by anyone regardless of class. Sable attunes to it during the long rest. She feels the item's frequency connect: close, but not perfectly aligned, as though she is not quite a steward in the way the item understands the word.
The Javelin of Lightning: Also recovered from the nest. A magic weapon requiring no attunement, taken by Lily. The Clockmaster's Apartment: The topmost floor sits firmly above the haze. Signs of past adventurers having camped here. The party finds several potions and takes a long rest safely.
Animal Messenger: Krenzo recruits a one-eyed rat named Bill via Animal Messenger and dispatches him to Emberwood Village with a message for Eldrick: "The clock tower is secure. Followers were here. Come quick. Signed, the Fate Benders." Bill departs by leaping off the top of the tower.
Malikas Arrives: Waking in the early hours, Sable investigates a sound below and discovers Malikas and three Amethyst Academy mages accompanied by Malikas already on site, quietly mending the damaged staircase.
The Party's Read on Eldrick: The party discusses what Eldrick's interest in the tower likely means. Clank notes that Eldrick seems more focused on Sable than anyone else. The tower's value as a safe rest point above the haze with apparent connections to ancient cosmological machinery makes it an obvious priority. The party reasons that removing the Garmyr serves everyone regardless of alliance.
The Return: Leaving Drakkenheim at dawn, Krenzo spots a creature watching the party from a rooftop: yellow eyes, long white curled fingers, crouched low. It notices him noticing it, fixes its gaze deliberately on Lily, then vanishes.
Ansom at the Gate: Ansom Lang meets the party as they emerge through the haze, visibly relieved. Sable reads him as genuine, but also quietly extracting information, and guilty enough about it to be obvious.
Petra's Briefing: Petra Lang hangs back to prepare the party before the audience with Elias Drexel. She mentions that Ash may currently be traveling with Moragon, both operating somewhere in the northern sector. She suggests Lily might be the one person who could convince him to come in from the field.
The Garrison: The party arrives at the Shepherd's Gate, a fortified city block of stone walls, ballista towers, barracks, and iron portcullises. The Hooded Lanterns drove out the monsters that occupied it the previous year and have been building it into their primary stronghold ever since.
Meeting Elias: Elias Drexel is a man in his early sixties. Tall, grey-templed, built like someone who has been dangerous his entire life and shows no signs of stopping. He greets the party warmly and immediately begins probing them for information. Sable reads him as a man who is not particularly gifted at hiding this.
The Queen: Clank confirms Queen Lenore is alive but mentally contaminated deep within the haze.
Flamekeeper Hannah: Flamekeeper Hannah of the Silver Order joins the conversation to propose a military alliance: the Silver Order will siege Temple Gate from the outside while the Hooded Lanterns strike the Garmyr from within, catching them in a pincer. Lord Commander Elias Drexel is unmoved.
The Gates: The party learns the full breakdown of Drakkenheim's five gates. Champions Gate is held by the Followers of the Fallen Flame. College Gate is completely destroyed, nearest to the crater. King's Gate is held by trolls. Shepherd's Gate is held by the Hooded Lanterns. Temple Gate is held by the Garmyr.
The Negotiation: The party becomes the deciding voice in the room. They work through the strategic case: the Garmyr are an existential threat to every faction in the city, Ash is actively fighting them in the field, and a pincer assault would be devastating if it lands. Lily pushes hard, invoking her son. The party also floats the Seals of Drakkenheim as a longer parallel path, used to perform a wish powerful enough to clear the Haze entirely.
The Deal: Lord Commander Elias Drexel agrees to muster forces within one month. His terms: Silver Order clerics prioritize Hooded Lanterns soldiers for contamination treatment after the assault. Flamekeeper Hannah accepts. The alliance is struck.
The Garmyr Attack: On the return through Drakkenheim, the party is ambushed. The figure seen from the rooftop had been marking Lily as quarry all along. The party defeats the attacking Garmyr force.
Lily Taken: During the fight, a Garmyr druid teleports Lily away. She awakens face to face with The Lord of the Feast. He has a specific and deliberate interest in her.
The Regroup: The rest of the party regroups at Shepherd's Gate, battered, and begins mustering help to track her down.
Sable attunes to the Steward's Seal.
Lily takes the Javelin of Lightning and is kidnapped by session's end. Currently face to face with The Lord of the Feast.
Clank meets Lord Commander Elias Drexel for the first time.
Steward's Seal: One of the Seals of Drakkenheim. Attuned by Sable.
Javelin of Lightning: Magic weapon, no attunement required. Held by Lily. Command word: "Tor." Potion of Growth × 1 Potion of Fire Breath × 1 Potion of Climbing × 1 Potion of Greater Healing × 1 Potion of Resistance × 1
53 gold — Recovered from the Crimson Countess's nest.
Malikas (Amethyst Academy): Arrived overnight to begin tower repairs.
Ansom Lang (Hooded Lanterns): Warm reception at Shepherd's Gate; visibly guilty about the intelligence he was extracting from Clank.
Petra Lang (Hooded Lanterns): Briefed the party before the audience; confirmed Ash may be in the field with Moragon.
Elias Drexel (Hooded Lanterns): First meeting; shaken by news of Queen Lenore; agreed to the alliance after the party made the case.
Flamekeeper Hannah (Silver Order): Brokered the alliance proposal; arrived from a position of weakness and needed the party to tip the room in her favour.
The Lord of the Feast (Garmyr): Lily awakens in his presence at session's end.
Bill (Rat): Godspeed.
Faction Thread: Securing the Cosmological Clock Tower
Status: Complete. Tower cleared. Amethyst Academy on site making repairs. Established as a safe house in the inner city.
Faction Thread: Find a Seal of Drakkenheim
Status: Advanced. Steward's Seal recovered and attuned by Sable.
Faction Thread: The Temple Gate Assault
Status: New. Alliance struck between the Silver Order and Hooded Lanterns. One month to muster. The party are involved as vanguard alongside Lightbringer Santanael.
Personal Thread: Finding Ash
Status: Advanced. Ash confirmed to possibly be traveling with Moragon, fighting the Garmyr in the northern sector.
Personal Thread: Finding Moragon
Status: Advanced. Moragon confirmed active in the field, possibly alongside Ash.

Act IV: Shepherd's Gate and the Lanterns

03-locations/bark-and-buzzard.html.title: Bark and Buzzard
03-locations/bark-and-buzzard.html.description: #locA small, one-level public house nestled quietly among the residential cottages of Emberwood Village. Known as a much more laid-back, peaceful, and localized alternative to the high-traffic chaos of the Red Lion Hotel, it is a favored spot for quiet drinking, low-profile information trading, and local brawls when tensions between rival adventurers inevitably boil over.

03-locations/black-ivory-inn.html.title: Black Ivory Inn
03-locations/black-ivory-inn.html.description:
#locAn unusual tavern stuck in a time loop, reliving the last moments of peace before the meteor fell. The more people eat, the more entranced they become until they can't leave the inn.
This time loop was created by Ms. Charlotte, a bard who continued to hold everybody captive to hear her music for decades. We discovered how to wound her and ended up breaking her spell when we destroyed her phylactery and shattered her protection.

03-locations/buckledown-row.html.title: Buckledown Row
03-locations/buckledown-row.html.description: #loc

03-locations/champion's-gate.html.title: Champion's Gate
03-locations/champion's-gate.html.description: #loc

03-locations/champions-gate.html.title: Champions Gate
03-locations/champions-gate.html.description:
#loc
One of the gates leading into the Inner City, currently held by the Followers of the Fallen Flame.

03-locations/college-gate.html.title: College Gate
03-locations/college-gate.html.description:
#loc
A destroyed gate that once led into the Inner City.

03-locations/cosmological-clocktower.htm.title: Cosmological Clocktower
03-locations/cosmological-clocktower.htm.description:
#loc
A towering structure, its uppermost level rises above the Haze.

03-locations/dran-river.html.title: Dran River
03-locations/dran-river.html.description:
#loc
A river running through Drakkenheim

03-locations/emberwood-village.html.title: Emberwood Village
03-locations/emberwood-village.html.description:
#loc
Emberwood Village is a small village in Westemär, located about 5 miles to the south of Drakkenheim.
Despite the wars that took place after Drakkenheim fell, and not to mention the sheer danger of simply being in close proximity to Drakkenheim, Emberwood Village has stubbornly clung to existence. In recent years it has even achieved a slim measure of renewed prosperity by serving as the de facto trade and logistics hub for the various factions, private concerns, and many adventurers and mercenaries operating within the city.

03-locations/inner-city.html.title: Inner City
03-locations/inner-city.html.description: #loc

03-locations/inscrutable-tower.html.title: Inscrutable Tower
03-locations/inscrutable-tower.html.description:
#loc
A place of arcane power within the Inner City of Drakkenheim.

03-locations/isle-of-sky.html.title: Isle of Sky
03-locations/isle-of-sky.html.description: #locThe elf homeland in Wastemar

03-locations/king's-gate.html.title: King's Gate
03-locations/king's-gate.html.description:
#loc
One of the gates leading into the Inner City, currently held by trolls.

03-locations/outer-city.html.title: Outer City
03-locations/outer-city.html.description: #loc

03-locations/red-lion-hotel.html.title: Red Lion Hotel
03-locations/red-lion-hotel.html.description:
#loc
A prominent, multi-story establishment in Emberwood Village that serves as a primary gathering place for travelers and mercenaries. Formerly a noble manor belonging to the village's local reeve before the meteor struck, it has since been converted into a bustling hotel and taproom. It provides a rare hot meal, a cold drink, and rentable rooms for those willing to pay a premium to rest between dangerous excursions into the Haze.

03-locations/shepherd's-gate.html.title: Shepherd's Gate
03-locations/shepherd's-gate.html.description:
#loc
One of the main gates to the Inner City, controlled by the Hooded Lanterns.

03-locations/temple-gate.html.title: Temple Gate
03-locations/temple-gate.html.description:
#loc
One of the gates leading into the Inner City, currently held by the Garmyr.

03-locations/the-obsidian-bank.html.title: The Obsidian Bank
03-locations/the-obsidian-bank.html.description: #loc

04-lore/amethyst-academy.html.title: Amethyst Academy
04-lore/amethyst-academy.html.description:
#lore
An elite syndicate of mages who hold a monopoly on arcane education and practices. They view Delirium not as a curse, but as an incredibly powerful, revolutionary resource for spellcasting and magical advancement. They are willing to pay top coin to adventurers who can smuggle raw Delirium out of the Haze for their research.
Eldrick Runeweaver, leader
River, researcher

04-lore/aqua-expurgo.html.title: Aqua Expurgo
04-lore/aqua-expurgo.html.description:
#lore
Grants characters temporary immunity to the detrimental, mutating effects of the Haze that blankets the ruined city of Drakkenheim. Haze Immunity: It provides vital protection against the toxic, magical contamination that adventurers face when exploring the ruins. This allows characters to delve into heavily contaminated zones for an extended period without having to make Contamination saving throws. This effect lasts for 8 hours.

04-lore/circle-of-contamination.html.title: Circle of Contamination
04-lore/circle-of-contamination.html.description: #lore

04-lore/circle-of-the-old-ways.html.title: Circle of the Old Ways
04-lore/circle-of-the-old-ways.html.description: #lore

04-lore/crimson-fist.html.title: Crimson Fist
04-lore/crimson-fist.html.description:
#loreA secretive order of vampiric monks.
Vorian, member

04-lore/delirium.html.title: Delirium
04-lore/delirium.html.description: #lore

04-lore/drakkenheim.html.title: Drakkenheim
04-lore/drakkenheim.html.description:
#lore #loc
What it all about

04-lore/followers-of-the-fallen-flame.ht.title: Followers of the Fallen Flame
04-lore/followers-of-the-fallen-flame.ht.description:
#lore
A radical sect that emerged after the meteor struck the city. They believe the Delirium meteor was not a disaster, but a holy herald of a cosmic prophecy. Led by Lucretia Mathias, they actively seek to commune with the cosmic elements, believing that embracing the corruption will enhance them.

04-lore/garmyr.html.title: Garmyr
04-lore/garmyr.html.description:
#loreDelerium-infested creatures living in the inner cirty. Deadly in numbers.
The Lord of the Feast, leader

04-lore/golden-order.html.title: Golden Order
04-lore/golden-order.html.description:
#lore
A region-spanning faction, offshoot of the Silver Order, focuses on the greater good over the particular events of Drakkenheim.
Kaelus Dawnstrider
Lightbringer Santanael

04-lore/hooded-lanterns.html.title: Hooded Lanterns
04-lore/hooded-lanterns.html.description:
#lore
The remnants of the city's old military guard and royal watch, led by Elias Drexel. Operating out of the old garrison and city gates, they are a lawful, disciplined faction dedicated to restoring order, rebuilding the city, clearing out monsters, and placing a rightful heir back onto the throne of Westemar.

04-lore/iron-accord.html.title: Iron Accord
04-lore/iron-accord.html.description:
#loreA mercenary group, known for their effective and ruthless nature.
Kavira, leader
Sable Emberlin, former member
Nikomai, member

04-lore/queen's-men.html.title: Queen's Men
04-lore/queen's-men.html.description:
#lore
A loose but powerful confederation of bandits, outlaws, scoundrels, and deserters who rule the lawless underbelly of the ruins. They view Drakkenheim as a lawless goldmine and seek to exploit the chaos and trade Delirium for maximum personal wealth and power.
Blackjack Mel, boss true identity of the Queen is a mystery to us

04-lore/seals-of-drakkenheim.html.title: Seals of Drakkenheim
04-lore/seals-of-drakkenheim.html.description:
#lore
Finding these seals is required to put an end to the political unrest of Drakkenheim.
Lord Commander's Badge (Lord Commander Elias Drexel): A badge of office held by the leader of the Hooded Lanterns. It allows the bearer to control the formidable gargoyles and tower dragons guarding the city. Chancellor's Crest: A silver pin depicting a hand holding a golden quill as if writing on parchment.
Steward's Seal: A brooch shaped like two hands presenting the seal of Drakkenheim. Spymaster's Signet: A small, golden ring inlaid with magical, intricate runes.
High Flame Keeper's Amulet (High Flame Keeper Ophelia Reed): A small silver chest (phylactery) containing the ashes of Saint Vitruvio. Inscrutable Staff (Archmage of the Inscrutable Tower): Powerful arcane relic somewhere inside the Inscrutable Tower.

04-lore/silver-order.html.title: Silver Order
04-lore/silver-order.html.description:
#lore
A militant order of mostly paladins and clerics. Driven by strict religious zeal, they view Delirium as a catastrophic, corrupting plague against creation. Their ultimate goal is to completely purge the city, contain the Haze, and destroy every last shard of Delirium by fire to protect the rest of the world.

04-lore/sinning-sons.html.title: Sinning Sons
04-lore/sinning-sons.html.description:
#loreA band of seedy mercenaries.
Nikomai, leader

04-lore/steward's-seal.html.title: Steward's Seal
04-lore/steward's-seal.html.description:
#lore
One of the Seals of Drakkenheim. Grants the wearer a suite of spells suited for hospitality endeavors.

04-lore/the-haze.html.title: the Haze
04-lore/the-haze.html.description: #lore

06-quests/felched-follower.html.title: Felched Follower
06-quests/felched-follower.html.description:
#quest
The party has contracted Eldrick to kidnap Sable's brother, Caelin.
Sable and Caelin have met, and he has agreed to remain at the Amethyst Academy, for now.

06-quests/find-a-seal-of-drakkenheim.htm.title: Find a Seal of Drakkenheim
06-quests/find-a-seal-of-drakkenheim.htm.description: #quest

06-quests/find-the-bagpipes.html.title: Find the Bagpipes
06-quests/find-the-bagpipes.html.description:
#quest
Dick's pipes are stuck somewhere in the Cosmological Clocktower.

06-quests/finding-ash.html.title: Finding Ash
06-quests/finding-ash.html.description:
#quest
The lone wolf Ash is believed to be in danger. Lily Willowthorn is trying to find her son.

06-quests/finding-moragon.html.title: Finding Moragon
06-quests/finding-moragon.html.description:
#quest
The missing druid Moragon may have the answers to Krenzo's questions.

06-quests/hell-hath-no-fury.html.title: Hell Hath No Fury
06-quests/hell-hath-no-fury.html.description:
#quest
Lily must find a way to legally separate from her husband, Tobin.

06-quests/hunting-the-pale-man.html.title: Hunting The Pale Man
06-quests/hunting-the-pale-man.html.description:
#quest
Believed to be working with Jenara and Jask, two figures from Krenzo's past, The Pale Man is believed to be performing horrific experiments on kidnapped victims.

06-quests/securing-the-cosmological-cloc.title: Securing the Cosmological Clock Tower
06-quests/securing-the-cosmological-cloc.description:
#quest
Eldrick has tasked us with securing the Cosmological Clocktower on behalf of the Amethyst Academy.
We reached the Cosmological Clocktower and defeated the Crimson Countess and her brood of harpies.

06-quests/the-mad-sovereign.html.title: The Mad Sovereign
06-quests/the-mad-sovereign.html.description:
#quest
Queen Lenore has tasked Clank with finding and delivering the Inscrutable Staff to her.

06-quests/the-temple-gate-assault.html.title: The Temple Gate Assault
06-quests/the-temple-gate-assault.html.description:
#quest
Broker an alliance between the Hooded Lanterns and Silver Order to attack the gate and end the Garmyr once and for all.

06-quests/tracking-soros.html.title: Tracking Soros
06-quests/tracking-soros.html.description:
#quest
Clank is seeking the whereabouts of his brother, Soros.

info_showDefaultFolderIcons.description: Show a default icon of a folder for every folder in the tree

info_showDefaultFileIcons.description: Show a default icon of a file for every file in the tree

info_defaultFolderIcon.description: The icon to use for folders. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultFileIcon.description: The icon to use for files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultMediaIcon.description: The icon to use for media files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_exposeStartingPath.description: Whether or not to show the current file in the file tree when the page is first loaded

info_minCollapseDepth.description: Only allow outline items to be collapsed if they are at least this many levels deep in the tree.

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_displayTitle.description: Descriptive title to show above the feature

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_displayTitle.description: Descriptive title to show above the feature

info_showOrphanNodes.description: Show nodes that are not connected to any other nodes.

info_showAttachments.description: Show attachments like images and PDFs as nodes in the graph.

info_allowGlobalGraph.description: Allow the user to view the global graph of all nodes.

info_allowExpand.description: Allow the user to pop-out the graph view to take up the whole screen

info_attractionForce.description: How much should linked nodes attract each other? This will make the graph appear more clustered.

info_linkLength.description: How long should the links between nodes be? The shorter the links the more connected nodes will cluster together.

info_repulsionForce.description: How much should nodes repel each other? This will make disconnected parts more spread out.

info_centralForce.description: How much should nodes be attracted to the center? This will make the graph appear more dense and circular.

info_edgePruning.description: Edges with a length above this threshold will not be rendered, however they will still contribute to the simulation. This can help large tangled graphs look more organised. Hovering over a node will still display these links.

info_minNodeRadius.description: How small should the smallest nodes be? The smaller a node is the less it will attract other nodes.

info_maxNodeRadius.description: How large should the largest nodes be? Nodes are sized by how many links they have. The larger a node is the more it will attract other nodes. This can be used to create a good grouping around the most important nodes.

info_allowResizing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be resized

info_allowCollapsing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be collapsed

info_rightDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the right sidebar

info_leftDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the left sidebar

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_sourcePath.description: The local path to the source .html file which will be included.

info_allowFoldingLists.description: Whether or not to allow lists to be folded

info_allowFoldingHeadings.description: Whether or not to allow headings to be folded

info_siteUrl.description: The url that this site will be hosted at

info_authorName.description: The name of the author of the site

bodyClasses: publish css-settings-manager styled-scrollbars show-inline-title show-ribbon is-focused
